diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Generalised itch is the defining symptom but is non-specific — it drives the exclusion work-up rather than confirming the cause.
Generalised pruritus without a rash mandates exclusion of renal/hepatic/thyroid/haematological causes.
Diffuse xerosis is the substrate for asteatotic itch and directly supports the xerotic mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Itch flares after hot showers and in heated dry winter air

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lipid-stripping and low humidity worsening the itch is characteristic of a barrier/xerotic cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No primary rash preceded the itch (itch-before-rash)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A primary vesicular/erythematous rash preceding the itch would be expected in contact dermatitis; its absence argues against it.
Itch-before-rash with only secondary excoriation fits a pruritus-driven (not primary-dermatosis) process.
No new medicine makes a drug-induced cause unlikely, though it does not fully exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Excoriations with pinpoint bleeding and secondary lichenification

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Secondary excoriation and lichenification are consequences of a chronic itch-scratch cycle; the pattern and any infection can only be judged on examination.
Broken bleeding skin is the portal of entry; examination is needed to detect early cellulitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of scabies burrows in web spaces/wrists/axillae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation of burrows (or their absence) is an examination finding that telehealth cannot obtain — required to exclude scabies.

symptoms.symptom_name: No scleral icterus, no jaundice, no peripheral oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of overt jaundice/oedema lowers but does not exclude occult hepatic/renal disease — bloods are still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with no spreading erythema, warmth or purulence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever or spreading cellulitic change at present argues against established secondary infection, but the risk remains and must be safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal baseline bloods (FBC, U&E/eGFR, LFTs, TFTs) — anticipated confirmatory work-up

investigation_details.investigation_name: Baseline bloods to exclude systemic pruritus (FBC, U&E/creatinine/eGFR, LFTs, TFTs, +/- glucose)
investigation_details.expected_result: Anticipated normal / no metabolic cause, which would support a diagnosis of exclusion (senile xerotic pruritus)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A silent uraemic, hepatic, thyroid or haematological cause of pruritus could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only bloods can exclude the silent systemic causes; unavailable via telehealth in real time.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Diffuse xerosis + hot-shower/heated-air aggravation + itch-before-rash together point to a barrier/xerotic mechanism rather than a primary dermatosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person skin examination (including web spaces/wrists for scabies) and assessment of excoriations
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms xerosis, excludes scabies, and assesses the excoriated skin for early infection — none achievable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline bloods: FBC, U&E/creatinine/eGFR, LFTs, TFTs (+/- glucose)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes silent uraemic, hepatic, thyroid and haematological causes of generalised pruritus.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate empirical anti-scabetic treatment without examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Empirically treating scabies remotely without confirming burrows is inappropriate — examination first.
